commands.description: Creates a new Minecraft installation with the specified loader and version.

parameters.name: string — display name for the installation
parameters.loader: string — one of: Fabric, Forge, Quilt, NeoForge, Vanilla

commands.description: Permanently deletes a Minecraft installation and all its files including mods, worlds, and config.

parameters.name: string — name of the installation to delete

commands.description: Searches Modrinth for a mod by name and installs it into the specified installation or server. Automatically resolves dependencies. If the mod is already installed, the download is skipped automatically.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the target installation OR server (use the exact name as it appears in the app)
parameters.modName: string — mod name or search term to look up on Modrinth

commands.description: Uninstalls a mod from an installation or server by name.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the installation or server
parameters.modName: string — display name of the mod to remove

commands.description: Creates a new Minecraft server.

parameters.name: string — server display name
parameters.serverType: string — one of: Vanilla, Fabric, Forge, Paper, Purpur, Spigot

commands.description: Starts a previously created server by name.

parameters.name: string — name of the server to start

commands.description: Stops a currently running server.

parameters.name: string — name of the server to stop

commands.description: Permanently deletes a server and all its world data and configuration.

parameters.name: string — name of the server to delete

commands.description: Launches a Minecraft installation (starts the game). Works in offline mode if no Microsoft account is signed in.

parameters.name: string — exact name of the installation to launch

commands.description: Scans all installed mods in an installation or server and reports missing or incompatible dependencies (e.g. GeckoLib, Kotlin for Forge, Architectury). Always run this after installing mods to catch issues before launching.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the installation or server to check

commands.description: Imports a local .jar file as a mod into an installation or server. Opens a file picker if no filePath is given. Use this for mods from CurseForge or other sources not on Modrinth.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the installation or server
parameters.filePath: string — absolute path to the .jar file (optional; opens file picker if omitted)

commands.description: Reads the latest log or most recent crash report for an installation or server. Use this to diagnose crashes, missing mods, or startup errors. Always use this before guessing what went wrong.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the installation or server
parameters.logType: string — 'latest' (default), 'crash' for the most recent crash report, or 'debug' for the Forge debug log
parameters.lines: integer — number of lines to return from the end of the log (default: 100, max: 500)

commands.description: Runs a full health check on an installation or server: directory structure, jar/run.bat presence, eula.txt, server.properties, and dependency compatibility. Use this when something isn't working and you don't know why.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the installation or server to verify

commands.description: Creates a zip snapshot of a server or installation (excluding the backups folder itself). Safe to run while the server is stopped.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the server or installation to back up

commands.description: Lists all available backup zips for a server or installation, with sizes and dates.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the server or installation

commands.description: Restores a previously created backup zip. The server must be stopped first. Use ListBackups to find the exact backup file name.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the server or installation
parameters.backupName: string — exact filename of the backup zip (e.g. 'backup_2026-07-01_14-30-00.zip')

commands.description: Reads a config file for a server or installation and updates a specific key=value pair. Defaults to server.properties for servers and options.txt for installations. If the server is running, live-reloadable properties (max-players, motd, pvp, difficulty, view-distance) are applied immediately.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the server or installation
parameters.key: string — the config key to change (e.g. 'max-players', 'view-distance', 'difficulty')
parameters.file: string — config filename relative to the server/instance directory (optional, defaults to server.properties / options.txt)

commands.description: Adds a player to the whitelist of a server. If the server is running, applies the change live without a restart.
Removes a player from the whitelist of a server.

parameters.player: string — Minecraft username to remove from whitelist

commands.description: Gives operator (admin) privileges to a player on a server. Applied live if the server is running.
Removes operator privileges from a player on a server. Applied live if the server is running.

Automatically tunes server.properties (view-distance, simulation-distance, max-tick-time, entity broadcast range) based on the server's RAM and type. For Paper/Purpur servers, also applies Aikar's JVM flags for best GC performance.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the server to optimize

commands.description: Searches Modrinth for mods that complement the current installation. Can be filtered by a theme (e.g. 'horror', 'performance', 'guns'). Only suggests mods not already installed.

parameters.theme: string — search theme or keyword (optional, e.g. 'performance', 'guns', 'horror')

commands.description: Scans the mod JARs of an installation or server for duplicate mod IDs (same mod loaded twice) and known incompatible mod pairs (e.g. OptiFine + Iris, WorldEdit + FAWE). More reliable than dependency checks for detecting runtime conflicts.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the installation or server

commands.description: Compares the mod list of a client installation with a server and reports mismatches (mods missing on server, mods only on server). Set autoInstall=true to automatically install missing client mods onto the server.

parameters.installationName: string — name of the client installation
parameters.autoInstall: string — 'true' to install missing mods automatically (default: 'false')

commands.description: Safely removes temporary files from an installation or server: cache/, logs/, crash-reports/, debug/, .fabric/ cache. Worlds, saves, screenshots, mods, and configs are NEVER touched.

parameters.targetName: string — name of the installation or server

commands.description: Reads the server log (debug.log or latest.log) and analyses it for TPS lag, entity overload, OutOfMemoryErrors, and exceptions. Returns a human-readable performance report with recommendations.

parameters.lines: integer — how many log lines to analyse from the end (default: 200)

commands.description: Switches an installation to a different mod loader (e.g. Forge → NeoForge, Vanilla → Fabric). Checks which installed mods have no compatible version for the new loader and warns before switching. Use force=true to switch anyway.

parameters.loader: string — target loader: Fabric, Forge, NeoForge, Quilt, Vanilla
parameters.force: string — 'true' to switch even if some mods are incompatible (default: 'false')

commands.description: Starts a public tunnel for a server so external players can join. Uses ngrok by default. Requires ngrok to be installed and an authtoken configured in Settings.

parameters.serverName: string — name of the server to expose
parameters.provider: string — tunnel provider to use: 'ngrok' (default), 'bore', 'serveo'. Optional.

commands.description: Stops an active public tunnel for a server.
